i: Note: Under Taliban governance (since August 2021), formal institutional channels are severely constrained. International NGOs operating in Afghanistan include WFP (World Food Programme), FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization), and ICRC (International Committee of the Red Cross). Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under sankcje constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council sankcje regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: kandydat do UE country with small open gospodarka ($27B PKB). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% PKB) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require środowiskowy acquis alignment, the nakładający się interes between oil producers and government revenue słabnie — EU środowiskowy standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession środowiskowy alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Gdy Albania must meet EU środowiskowy standards, nakładający się interes w continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.
Wdrożyć EU-aligned środowiskowy monitoring and emisje disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to emisje makes W visible.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for środowiskowy monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts środowiskowy impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's sygnalista protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's państwowe oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and górnictwo revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent regulator energii, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant energia odnawialna expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and środowiskowy legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and środowiskowy acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked gospodarka ($26B PKB) with significant górnictwo sector (min=6.79% of PKB, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% PKB). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's górnictwo sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Gdy downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W staje się obliczalne from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate środowiskowy bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Gdy górnictwo companies must pre-fund środowiskowy remediation, nakładający się interes w extraction without cleanup słabnie — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all górnictwo operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to środowiskowy monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Częściowo łamalne. Open-pit górnictwo inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of zależność systemowa.

i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with środowiskowy regulations at górnictwo operations, conducting inspections, and imposing sankcje. Ministry of Environment — oversees środowiskowy policy, issues środowiskowy impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the górnictwo sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest górnictwo company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of górnictwo revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees górnictwo policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the górnictwo sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and społeczeństwo obywatelskie representatives overseeing extractive sector przejrzystość.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on środowiskowy protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the gospodarka. Agriculture (22.1% PKB) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the nakładający się interes between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) słabnie — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to energia odnawialna and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Gdy renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the nakładający się interes breaks.

Wdrożyć EITI-standard revenue przejrzystość extended to per-block środowiskowy impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to emisje and środowiskowy costs makes W computable per production unit.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for środowiskowy policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced przejrzystość standards.

AR.s: G20 gospodarka ($638.4B PKB) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag PKB share but outsized środowiskowy footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the nakładający się interes — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to energia odnawialna. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Gdy renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the nakładający się interes between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well emisje disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to emisje makes W computable from production statistics.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.

i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's organ ochrony środowiska, responsible for środowiskowy permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial środowiskowy coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal sygnalista protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% państwowe), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for środowiskowy policy and zmiana klimatu. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting emisje of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU gospodarka ($534.8B PKB) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of PKB). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% PKB) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Gdy per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W staje się obliczalne from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Wdrożyć front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Gdy consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the nakładający się interes between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products słabnie.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Zreformowaćulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate zależność systemowa.

i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent gospodarka with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited społeczeństwo obywatelskie space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the nakładający się interes between SOCAR and international partners (BP) słabnie — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance energia odnawialna and non-oil economic development. Gdy SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the nakładający się interes breaks.
Wdrożyć na złoże emisje disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes emisje data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's organ ochrony środowiska, responsible for środowiskowy monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees środowiskowy impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive sygnalista protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent przejrzystość mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs środowiskowy standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and środowiskowy issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict gospodarka ($29.6B PKB) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% PKB). kandydat do UE country. Significant coal górnictwo and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the nakładający się interes between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Gdy Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The nakładający się interes between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory emisje monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned środowiskowy reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Nie do złamania. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.

i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate środowiskowy agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — środowiskowy inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive sygnalista protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in górnictwo communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and środowiskowy acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large gospodarka ($450.1B PKB) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of PKB). High agricultural employment (11.2% PKB). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Zreformować agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Gdy subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, nakładający się interes w monoculture between farmers and input suppliers słabnie.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Częściowo łamalne. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce zależność systemowa.

i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive sygnalista protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Opracowaćment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Opracowaćment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's środowiskowy regulatory agency, responsible for środowiskowy clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters gospodarka ($671.4B PKB). Services-dominant (71% PKB). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% PKB). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Wdrożyć front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information słabnie nakładający się interes w high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Zreformowaćulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate zależność systemowa.

i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian gospodarka ($23.1B PKB) with dominant gold górnictwo sector (min=15.45% PKB). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% PKB). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold górnictwo generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal górnictwo), and community displacement. Gdy środowiskowy and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W staje się obliczalne.
bp: Mandate środowiskowy bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial górnictwo licenses. Gdy górnictwo companies must pre-fund środowiskowy remediation and community compensation, nakładający się interes w extraction without accountability słabnie.

Publish na kopalnię środowiskowy impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to środowiskowy data makes W visible.

Częściowo łamalne. Open-pit gold górnictwo inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce zależność systemowa.

i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national środowiskowy assessment authority, responsible for środowiskowy impact assessments of górnictwo projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees środowiskowy policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and społeczeństwo obywatelskie space is restricted.

Major górnictwo companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international górnictwo standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for górnictwo policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and górnictwo technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on górnictwo revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes środowiskowy obligations for górnictwo companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable górnictwo governance.

BG.s: członek UE gospodarka ($113.3B PKB) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% PKB). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the nakładający się interes between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Gdy Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The nakładający się interes breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory na elektrownię emisje and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Nie do złamania. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.

i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional środowiskowy agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national środowiskowy monitoring agency collecting emisje and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the państwowe lignite górnictwo company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent regulator energii, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B PKB) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the nakładający się interes breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC gospodarka. Gdy financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, nakładający się interes w extraction breaks.
Publish na złoże środowiskowy and emisje data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle emisje visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's organ ochrony środowiska, responsible for środowiskowy policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees środowiskowy impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the państwowe oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited sygnalista protection; społeczeństwo obywatelskie space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Opracowaćment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a monarchia konstytucyjna with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African gospodarka ($21.5B PKB) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% PKB, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% PKB). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Gdy soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W staje się obliczalne from agricultural output data.
bp: Zreformować cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Gdy input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the nakładający się interes between cotton traders and input suppliers słabnie.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making środowiskowy and health costs visible makes W computable.

Częściowo łamalne. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce zależność systemowa — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.

i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's środowiskowy agency, responsible for środowiskowy impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of społeczeństwo obywatelskie organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts środowiskowy monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B PKB) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the gospodarka. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the nakładający się interes between BSP and the government in continued extraction słabnie — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Gdy non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, nakładający się interes w extraction breaks.
Wdrożyć na złoże emisje disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Opracowaćment, responsible for środowiskowy policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no sygnalista protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on zmiana klimatu provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent gospodarka ($54.9B PKB) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and górnictwo (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Rolnictwo na poziomie 8.8% PKB with soy expansion driving wylesianie. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Gdy water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W staje się obliczalne.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Gdy indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, nakładający się interes w extraction without consent słabnie.

Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making środowiskowy costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Częściowo łamalne. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.

i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the górnictwo administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing górnictwo concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and środowiskowy rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state górnictwo corporation overseeing Bolivia's górnictwo sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's górnictwo ministry, responsible for górnictwo policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing środowiskowy policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian demokracja. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island gospodarka ($15.8B PKB) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of PKB).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Gdy beneficial ownership information and tax przejrzystość data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Wdrożyć full beneficial ownership przejrzystość in line with FATF recommendations. Gdy the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through przejrzystość, the nakładający się interes between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients słabnie.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
Częściowo łamalne. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.

i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Usługi Finansowe Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style parlamentarny demokracja. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income gospodarka ($19.4B PKB) with diamond górnictwo historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% PKB). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the nakładający się interes between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium słabnie — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Gdy non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, nakładający się interes w continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle środowiskowy data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all górnictwo operations. Making środowiskowy costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Częściowo łamalne. Open-pit diamond górnictwo inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term zależność systemowa.

i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for środowiskowy policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for górnictwo safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees górnictwo policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the parlamentarny website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including górnictwo.

BY.s: State-controlled gospodarka ($76B PKB) with significant manufacturing (20.3% PKB). Potash górnictwo (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash górnictwo generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Gdy downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W staje się obliczalne.
bp: International sankcje (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the nakładający się interes by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Gdy sanctioned export revenues fall below środowiskowy remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based środowiskowy monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making środowiskowy damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
Częściowo łamalne. Potash górnictwo inherently generates salt waste. However, solution górnictwo and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.

i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, społeczeństwo obywatelskie, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective sygnalista protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international środowiskowy organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's państwowe potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK sankcje since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under autorytarny governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU sankcje framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including środowiskowy governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished gospodarka ($71B PKB). Mining dominates (28.81% of PKB) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of PKB.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Gdy supply chain traceability makes mine-level środowiskowy and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W staje się obliczalne from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Gdy artisanal miners must meet labor and środowiskowy standards to access global markets, nakładający się interes w unregulated extraction słabnie.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking na kopalnię środowiskowy and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Częściowo łamalne. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal górnictwo with proper safety and środowiskowy management can reduce zależność systemowa compared to unregulated extraction.

i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale górnictwo. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping górnictwo sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees górnictwo policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for górnictwo sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent gospodarka ($15.7B PKB) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the nakładający się interes between international operators and government revenue słabnie — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Gdy non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, nakładający się interes w continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Wdrożyć EITI-standard per-block revenue and emisje disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to środowiskowy data makes W computable per production unit.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest gospodarka in francophone West Africa ($87.1B PKB). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of PKB with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require wylesianie-free supply chains — when per-farm wylesianie data is linked to cocoa transactions, W staje się obliczalne.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Gdy cocoa buyers must verify zero-wylesianie, the nakładający się interes between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm środowiskowy data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Częściowo łamalne. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal wylesianie for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Wdrożyć full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Inwestować w cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Przyjąć living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Egzekwować traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to środowiskowy compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Wzmocnić enforcement against wylesianie in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Skontaktuj się ze swoim Député in the Assemblée nationale by zażądać enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Wspierać przez the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding wylesianie targets
Naciskać na living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end wylesianie in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and środowiskowy regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to wylesianie after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income gospodarka ($330.3B PKB) with massive górnictwo sector (16.23% of PKB). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium górnictwo depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Gdy na kopalnię water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W staje się obliczalne from górnictwo output data.
bp: Wdrożyć water pricing that reflects true scarcity in górnictwo regions. Gdy miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, nakładający się interes w water-intensive extraction methods słabnie — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory na kopalnię water balance, tailings chemistry, and emisje disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects środowiskowy data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Częściowo łamalne. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report środowiskowy violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's sygnalista law (Ley 21.015) and środowiskowy complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Przyjąć Science Based Targets for emisje reduction across górnictwo operations
Wdrożyć ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase środowiskowy monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at górnictwo operations
Sernageomin: Wzmocnić tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Wdrożyć the National Lithium Strategy with binding środowiskowy standards
Skontaktuj się ze swoim Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional by zażądać mandatory water-neutral górnictwo operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on środowiskowy access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for górnictwo supply chains
Naciskać na international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's środowiskowy enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with środowiskowy permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts środowiskowy impact assessments for górnictwo projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's państwowe copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper górnictwo companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and górnictwo service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees górnictwo policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African gospodarka ($53.3B PKB) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% PKB — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the nakładający się interes between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue słabnie — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Gdy REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, nakładający się interes w continued extraction breaks.
Wdrożyć EITI-standard per-block środowiskowy disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to środowiskowy data makes W computable.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report środowiskowy violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Opracowaćment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Wdrożyć środowiskowy monitoring and emisje reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Opracować post-oil diversification strategy
Przyjąć TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Wzmocnić środowiskowy conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Egzekwować środowiskowy impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to środowiskowy data
Skontaktuj się ze swoim Député in the Assemblée nationale by zażądać transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Wspierać przez CEMAC for regional środowiskowy standards
Naciskać na EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees górnictwo and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American gospodarka ($418.8B PKB) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, nakładający się interes w fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in energia odnawialna. Gdy energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, nakładający się interes w continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory na złoże emisje disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emisje makes W computable.
Nie do złamania by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
